st_before_fix:
FUNCTION_BLOCK SelectSort
VAR_INPUT
    exe : BOOL;          // Trigger to start sorting
    seq : VARIANT;      // Array to be sorted
END_VAR

VAR_OUTPUT
    error : BOOL;       // Indicates if an error occurred
    status : WORD;      // Status code reflecting the outcome
END_VAR

VAR
    arrayLength : INT;  // Length of the array
    i : INT;            // Loop variable for outer loop
    j : INT;            // Loop variable for inner loop
    minIndex : INT;     // Index of the minimum element
    temp : VARIANT;     // Temporary variable for swapping
END_VAR

// Initialization
error := FALSE;         // Default to no error
status := 16#0000;     // Default status code

IF exe THEN
    // Check if seq is an array type
    IF NOT (TypeOf(seq) = 'ARRAY') THEN
        error := TRUE;
        status := 16#8A01; // Invalid array type
    ELSE
        // Get the length of the array
        arrayLength := ArrayLength(seq);
        
        // Selection sort algorithm
        FOR i := 0 TO arrayLength - 2 DO
            minIndex := i; // Assume the first element is the minimum
            
            FOR j := i + 1 TO arrayLength - 1 DO
                // Compare and find the minimum element
                IF seq[j] < seq[minIndex] THEN
                    minIndex := j; // Update minIndex if a smaller element is found
                END_IF
            END_FOR
            
            // Swap the found minimum element with the first unsorted element
            IF minIndex <> i THEN
                temp := seq[i];
                seq[i] := seq[minIndex];
                seq[minIndex] := temp;
            END_IF
        END_FOR
        
        // Sorting completed successfully
        error := FALSE;
        status := 16#0000; // No error
    END_IF
END_IF

END_FUNCTION_BLOCK

```plaintext
- Fix suggestion 1: The type 'VARIANT' is not recognized in the context of IEC 61131-3. Instead, use a specific array type such as 'ARRAY[0..N] OF INT' or 'ARRAY[0..N] OF REAL' depending on the intended data type for sorting.
- Fix suggestion 2: Ensure that the array length and type are properly defined to avoid runtime errors when accessing elements.

(1)

VAR_INPUT

    seq : VARIANT;      // Array to be sorted
END_VAR

VAR_OUTPUT

    status : WORD;      // Status code reflecting the outcome
END_VAR

VAR
    arrayLength : INT;  // Length of the array
    i : INT;            // Loop variable for outer loop
    j : INT;            // Loop variable for inner loop

    temp : VARIANT;     // Temporary variable for swapping
END_VAR

// Initialization
error := FALSE;         // Default to no error
status := 16#0000;     // Default status code

IF exe THEN
    // Check if seq is an array type
    IF NOT (TypeOf(seq) = 'ARRAY') THEN
        error := TRUE;
        status := 16#8A01; // Invalid array type
    ELSE

        // Selection sort algorithm

                seq[minIndex] := temp;

        status := 16#0000; // No error

END_IF

END_FUNCTION_BLOCK
</code_segment>
<patch>
FUNCTION_BLOCK SelectSort
VAR_INPUT
    exe : BOOL;                      // Trigger to start sorting
    seq : ARRAY[0..N] OF INT;       // Array to be sorted (replace N with the actual size)
END_VAR

VAR_OUTPUT
    error : BOOL;                   // Indicates if an error occurred
    status : WORD;                  // Status code reflecting the outcome
END_VAR

VAR
    arrayLength : INT;              // Length of the array
    i : INT;                        // Loop variable for outer loop
    j : INT;                        // Loop variable for inner loop
    minIndex : INT;                 // Index of the minimum element
    temp : INT;                     // Temporary variable for swapping
END_VAR

// Initialization
error := FALSE;                     // Default to no error
status := 16#0000;                 // Default status code

IF exe THEN
    // Get the length of the array
    arrayLength := SIZEOF(seq) / SIZEOF(seq[0]); // Calculate the length of the array
    
    // Selection sort algorithm
    FOR i := 0 TO arrayLength - 2 DO
        minIndex := i; // Assume the first element is the minimum
        
        FOR j := i + 1 TO arrayLength - 1 DO
            // Compare and find the minimum element
            IF seq[j] < seq[minIndex] THEN
                minIndex := j; // Update minIndex if a smaller element is found
            END_IF
        END_FOR
        
        // Swap the found minimum element with the first unsorted element
        IF minIndex <> i THEN
            temp := seq[i];
            seq[i] := seq[minIndex];
            seq[minIndex] := temp;
        END_IF
    END_FOR
    
    // Sorting completed successfully
    error := FALSE;
    status := 16#0000; // No error
END_IF

END_FUNCTION_BLOCK
</patch>
```

FUNCTION_BLOCK SelectSort
VAR_INPUT
    exe : BOOL;                      // Trigger to start sorting
```plaintext
    seq : ARRAY[0..10] OF INT;      // Array to be sorted (replace 10 with the actual size)
END_VAR

    error : BOOL;                   // Indicates if an error occurred
    status : WORD;                  // Status code reflecting the outcome

    arrayLength : INT;              // Length of the array
    i : INT;                        // Loop variable for outer loop
    j : INT;                        // Loop variable for inner loop
    minIndex : INT;                 // Index of the minimum element
    temp : INT;                     // Temporary variable for swapping
END_VAR

// Initialization
error := FALSE;                     // Default to no error
status := 16#0000;                 // Default status code

IF exe THEN
    // Get the length of the array
    arrayLength := SIZEOF(seq) / SIZEOF(seq[0]); // Calculate the length of the array
    
    // Selection sort algorithm
    FOR i := 0 TO arrayLength - 2 DO
        minIndex := i; // Assume the first element is the minimum
        
        FOR j := i + 1 TO arrayLength - 1 DO
            // Compare and find the minimum element
            IF seq[j] < seq[minIndex] THEN
                minIndex := j; // Update minIndex if a smaller element is found
            END_IF
        END_FOR
        
        // Swap the found minimum element with the first unsorted element
        IF minIndex <> i THEN
            temp := seq[i];
            seq[i] := seq[minIndex];
            seq[minIndex] := temp;
        END_IF
    END_FOR
    
    // Sorting completed successfully
    error := FALSE;
    status := 16#0000; // No error
END_IF

END_FUNCTION_BLOCK
```
